stories.headline: Mindanao quake death toll reaches 37 as over 31,000 shelter in evacuation centers
stories.summary: The Office of Civil Defense said deaths from Monday's magnitude 7.8 offshore earthquake near Sarangani have risen to 37, with 33 recorded in Soccsksargen and four in the Davao Region. Officials reported 488 injured and four missing, while more than 8,700 families are staying in 54 evacuation centers. Search and retrieval operations are concentrated in General Santos City, which has been placed under a state of calamity, and in Sarangani province.
stories.context: The magnitude 7.8 quake on June 8 was the strongest to hit Mindanao since the 1976 Moro Gulf earthquake, and follows a series of destructive quakes in the Davao region in late 2023.
stories.watch_for: PHIVOLCS has warned of aftershocks above magnitude 6 in the coming days; the OCD is due to release an updated casualty and damage bulletin this evening.

stories.headline: 157,000 consumers still without power as quake-hit plants stay offline
stories.summary: The Department of Energy said around 157,000 consumers remained without electricity after the Sarangani earthquake, with two power plants in Davao Occidental and Sarangani still offline after automatically tripping, removing roughly 832 megawatts of generation. Repairs are ongoing on the Tacurong-General Santos transmission lines. Tsunami alerts triggered by the quake in several coastal provinces were lifted on June 8.

stories.headline: Senate shifts to remote work over reported security threat as leadership feud deepens
stories.summary: Acting Senate President Sherwin Gatchalian ordered Senate employees to work from home on June 10 and 11 to allow a security assessment of the chamber's premises, citing threat information from the NBI. Senator Alan Peter Cayetano, who was ousted in the June 3 leadership change, questioned the intelligence's credibility and issued a contradicting work advisory, while Malacanang and the House continue to recognize the Gatchalian bloc.
stories.context: The Senate has had two rival leadership blocs since the June 3 ouster of Cayetano, with both camps claiming legitimacy and issuing competing orders to the chamber's staff.
stories.watch_for: The Supreme Court is expected to act this week on a petition asking it to rule on which leadership election was valid.

stories.headline: Diesel up P5.77 per liter as fuel prices climb again this week
stories.summary: Oil firms raised pump prices effective Tuesday, June 9, reversing the previous week's large rollback, with diesel and kerosene rising by more than P4 per liter under advisories from Seaoil, Shell, and Unioil, and diesel increases reaching P5.77 in some advisories. The Department of Energy said national fuel inventory stands at about 47 days, with the increases attributed to renewed volatility in global crude markets tied to stalled Middle East negotiations.
stories.watch_for: Oil firms announce next week's price adjustments on Monday; analysts expect another increase if Middle East supply talks remain stalled.

stories.headline: Southwest monsoon brings rain to western Luzon; thunderstorm advisories up in Visayas
stories.summary: PAGASA said the southwest monsoon is affecting the western section of Northern Luzon, while localized thunderstorms will bring rain showers to much of the rest of the country. Thunderstorm advisories were issued early Wednesday for parts of Cebu, Bohol, Leyte, and Palawan, with warnings of possible flash floods and landslides in affected areas.

stories.headline: Viewers' verdict splits online as "Sandigan ng Puso" finale airs unedited cut & extended ending
stories.summary: The primetime series "Sandigan ng Puso" wrapped its three-year run with a double-length finale that trended nationwide within minutes of airing. The network said an extended director's cut will stream over the weekend, while cast members thanked fans at a send-off event in Quezon City.
